scenario_results.name: Managed PostgreSQL platform shortlist
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves source-backed cloud database pattern shortlisting, RTO/RPO evidence handling, restore-drill gap identification, cost and lock-in tradeoffs, and no application schema drift.

scenario_results.name: Backup and restore feasibility gap
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves backup, restore, PITR, retention, restore-test evidence, validation gates, and runbook output fidelity under provider-source constraints.

scenario_results.name: Resiliency and failover ADR
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves failover ADR output, zone and regional failure-domain reasoning, residency caveats, source use, and validation requirements.

scenario_results.name: IOPS and storage bottleneck triage
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves operations troubleshooting, telemetry prerequisite handling, root-cause hypothesis framing, severity mapping, and refusal of query rewrite ownership.

scenario_results.name: Cross-provider migration rollout and rollback
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves migration sequencing, cutover gates, rollback checkpoints, data validation, provider assumption handling, and owner handoffs.

scenario_results.name: Database incident checklist and observability gaps
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves incident checklist output, severity mapping, missing telemetry registration, working evidence capture, and incident-command boundary handling.

scenario_results.name: IaC database guardrail review
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves IaC and configuration checkpoint output for backup retention, HA, deletion protection, maintenance, alarms, provider-version evidence, and no live execution authority.

scenario_results.name: Cost, reliability, and isolation tradeoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cost, reliability, isolation, performance, recovery, staged adoption, and risk-acceptance boundary coverage.

scenario_results.name: Accepted execution through completion evidence
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves execution lifecycle progression from accepted intake through evidence review, runbook production, completion evidence, and disposition summary.

scenario_results.name: Processing blocked by missing approval or upstream records
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves exception queue behavior when RTO/RPO, change calendar, backup vault access, approvals, and evidence are missing.

scenario_results.name: Application SQL model and schema migration refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of application SQL model design, schema migration scripts, query optimization, and appropriate routing.

scenario_results.name: Application code, ORM, and API data-access refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of application code refactoring, ORM configuration, and API-layer data-access architecture.

scenario_results.name: Identity-only governance and endpoint hardening refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of identity-only governance, IAM policy design, and endpoint hardening as primary concerns.

scenario_results.name: Vendor negotiation and exact pricing refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of vendor negotiation, exact pricing quotes, procurement, and spend approval while preserving technical handoff context.

scenario_results.name: Malware, credential abuse, and breach-response refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of malware forensics, credential-abuse investigation, breach response, customer notification, legal interpretation, and incident command.

scenario_results.name: Low-confidence missing tenant facts
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves low-confidence escalation when workload, provider, engine, topology, RTO/RPO, compliance, backup, monitoring, and owner facts are missing.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting public source, tenant policy, and contract
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves conflicting-source and conflicting-policy escalation with source precedence, legal/compliance ownership, and no confident synthesis.

scenario_results.name: Industry-specific records required before adjacent routing
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cross-industry artifact awareness, refusal to treat the industry name as cosmetic, named records requirement, explicit boundary statements, and adjacent specialist routing.

notes: All 18 refreshed scenarios passed the standard 0.90 threshold while preserving the database platform operations specialty and expanding research, execution-workflow, output-fidelity, boundary, and functionality coverage.
Functionality coverage includes six shared abilities, three execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
Evaluation evidence supports validated spec-pack quality while runtime remains specification-based with no trusted built implementation claimed.
